methods.description: This method involves statistical procedures to analyze diatom assemblages in sediment cores to understand changes over time. It uses squared chord distance (SCD) dissimilarity scores and breakpoint analysis to evaluate changes in diatom assemblages and principal components analysis (PCA) for visualization.

steps.description: Squared chord distance (SCD) dissimilarity scores were determined using R for cores BAIK13-7A_SB, BAIK13-11C_BS and BAIK13-18A_NB.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate SCD dissimilarity scores using R

steps.description: Breakpoint analysis was carried out on the SCD scores in R using the segmented library.
steps.searchable_summary: perform breakpoint analysis using segmented library in R

steps.description: Temporal changes in the diatom assemblages were visualised using principal components analysis (PCA) of major taxa.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCA for visualization of diatom assemblages

steps.description: Data were reanalysed using PCA with square root transformation of species abundances using Canoco 4.5.
steps.searchable_summary: reanalyze data using PCA with square root transformation in Canoco 4.5

methods.description: This method involves analyzing surface sediment samples for diatoms, calculating diatom concentrations, and estimating diatom dissolution to understand sedimentary diatom assemblages.

steps.description: Surface sediment samples (upper 2 cm) were analysed for diatoms.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze surface sediment samples for diatoms

steps.description: Diatom concentrations were calculated by adding a known weight of divinylbenzene microspheres to the cleaned samples.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate diatom concentrations using divinylbenzene microspheres

steps.description: Subsamples of the suspensions were diluted and settled out onto coverslips and fixed onto slides with Naphrax.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare slides with diluted subsamples using Naphrax

steps.description: A total of 300 valves were counted at x1000 magnification using an oil immersion lens and phase contrast under a Zeiss Axioskop 2 plus light microscope.
Diatom dissolution was estimated by categorising endemic diatom species into three stages of valve preservation.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate diatom dissolution by categorizing valve preservation

steps.description: A diatom dissolution index (DDI) was calculated to quantify the extent of diatom preservation in every sample.
